The Daily Brew

Good morning. It's Friday, which means we've survived another week of market chaos. Let's get into it.

Markets

Together with NordVPN. Your data is worth more than you think — and advertisers are paying for it. NordVPN encrypts your traffic so trackers can't follow you. Brew readers get 70% off a 2-year plan. Claim the deal.

Tech

OpenAI ships a smaller, faster reasoning model

OpenAI released o4-mini yesterday, a distilled reasoning model that runs at roughly a third of the cost of o4 while matching it on most coding benchmarks. The move pressures Anthropic and Google to cut prices on their mid-tier models. Engineers are already reporting that the smaller context window (64k) is the main trade-off. Read more.

High-speed rail breaks ground in the Central Valley

California's long-delayed high-speed rail project finally laid its first section of electrified track between Merced and Bakersfield. The 171-mile segment is now slated to carry passengers by 2031. Critics note the full LA-to-SF line remains unfunded past this initial spine. Full story.

Quick Hits

  • A new small modular reactor design got NRC design certification — the third ever. (More)
  • Nintendo confirmed the Switch 2 sold 6 million units in its first month.
  • The EU's AI Act enforcement office opened in Brussels.
